name: Layered Custard Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. For pourable fruit, substitute 6 tablespoons peach fruit spread mixed with 2 tablespoons warm water. If almond liqueur is unavailable, use 2 tablespoons apple juice concentrate and 1/2 teaspoon almond extract.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
3. Combine flour and salt in a medium bowl. Cut butter into the mixture using a pastry blender or two knives until it resembles coarse crumbs.
4. Add liqueur; mix well.
5. Press dough evenly onto the bottom of an 8-inch square baking pan.
6. Bake for 15 minutes, until set.
7. Arrange peaches evenly over partially baked crust. If using peach halves, cut them into slices first.
8. Combine eggs and pourable fruit in a bowl; mix until well blended.
9. Pour mixture evenly over the peaches.
10. Melt butter; add almonds and cinnamon, mixing lightly.
11. Sprinkle almond mixture evenly over the egg mixture.
12. Bake for 20-25 minutes, until almonds are golden brown and custard is set.
13. Cool completely on a wire rack.
14. Cut into bars. Serve at room temperature or chilled. Refrigerate leftover bars.
